name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 06.10.2025 .

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 06.10.2025 ..pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 03 October, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1121.46 lakh ha as compared to 1114.95 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 102.3 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 03 October, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1121.46 lakh ha as compared to 1114.95 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 102.3 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be below normal in most of India except some parts of Tamil Nadu and South Karnataka.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be below normal in most of India except some parts of Tamil Nadu and South Karnataka.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From October 01 -October 05 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  58%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  136%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  192%;  (iii)  Central  India:  62%;  and  South Peninsula: (-) 50%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From October 01 -October 05 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  58%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  136%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  192%;  (iii)  Central  India:  62%;  and  South Peninsula: (-) 50%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to very heavy rainfall with possibility of extremely heavy rainfall likely over Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and Meghalaya today, the 05 th  October, 2025.
texts.text: Heavy to very heavy rainfall with possibility of extremely heavy rainfall likely over Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and Meghalaya today, the 05 th  October, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · A fresh intense western disturbance is likely cause heavy to very heavy rainfall spell over Northwest India during 05 th  - 07 th  October with peak intensity on 06 th  October.
texts.text: A fresh intense western disturbance is likely cause heavy to very heavy rainfall spell over Northwest India during 05 th  - 07 th  October with peak intensity on 06 th  October.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated  extremely  heavy  rainfall  ( ≥ 21  cm)  likely  over  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  & Sikkim on 05 th  October.
texts.text: Isolated  extremely  heavy  rainfall  ( ≥ 21  cm)  likely  over  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  & Sikkim on 05 th  October.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over Bihar on 05 th  and heavy rainfall over Gangetic West Bengal on 05 th  and Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim on 06 th  October.
texts.text: Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over Bihar on 05 th  and heavy rainfall over Gangetic West Bengal on 05 th  and Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim on 06 th  October.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Light to moderate rainfall/thunderstorm at most/many places with isolated thunderstorm  &  gusty  winds  very  likely  over  East  India  during  next  3-4  days.  Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall activity is likely over Northeast India.
texts.text: Light to moderate rainfall/thunderstorm at most/many places with isolated thunderstorm  &  gusty  winds  very  likely  over  East  India  during  next  3-4  days.  Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall activity is likely over Northeast India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over West & East India.
texts.text: Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over West & East India.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 02 October 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 164.99 billion cubic meter, which is 90.4% of total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 103.5 percent of last year's storage and 115.0 percent of the average storage of the last ten years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better  than  the
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 02 October 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 164.99 billion cubic meter, which is 90.4% of total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 103.5 percent of last year's storage and 115.0 percent of the average storage of the last ten years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better  than  the

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Paddy (Rice Hispa)) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Paddy (Rice Hispa)) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (25 Sept -01 Oct 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 28%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  30 th Sept  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 8%.
texts.text: During the week (25 Sept -01 Oct 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 28%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  30 th Sept  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 8%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  east  &  northeast  India  and Chhattisgarh; and below normal over remaining parts of the country during the week.
texts.text: Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  east  &  northeast  India  and Chhattisgarh; and below normal over remaining parts of the country during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025
texts.text: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Southwest  monsoon  has  withdrawal  from  many  parts  of  Gujarat  and  Rajasthan;  entire Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh & Delhi; some parts of Madhya Pradesh, UP, Himachal Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir till now.
texts.text: Southwest  monsoon  has  withdrawal  from  many  parts  of  Gujarat  and  Rajasthan;  entire Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh & Delhi; some parts of Madhya Pradesh, UP, Himachal Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir till now.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The line of withdrawal of southwest monsoon continues to pass through Veraval, Bharuch, Ujjain, Jhansi and Shahjahanpur.
texts.text: The line of withdrawal of southwest monsoon continues to pass through Veraval, Bharuch, Ujjain, Jhansi and Shahjahanpur.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Conditions are favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon from some more parts of Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh and some parts of Maharashtra during next 45 days.
texts.text: Conditions are favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon from some more parts of Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh and some parts of Maharashtra during next 45 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION
texts.text: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Soil Moisture Observation
texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 25 Sept - 01 Oct 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Karnataka,  Kerala,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 25 Sept - 01 Oct 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Karnataka,  Kerala,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of northern parts of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam, in parts of Jharkhand and in parts of Tamil Nadu.
texts.text: It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of northern parts of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam, in parts of Jharkhand and in parts of Tamil Nadu.

texts.text: 5.   CROP SOWING STATUS FOR KHARIF SEASON

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Kharif Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rice :  About  441.58  lakh  ha  (109.5%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 435.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rice :  About  441.58  lakh  ha  (109.5%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 435.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Moong : About 34.87 lakh ha (97.6% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Moong : About 34.87 lakh ha (97.6% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soybean : About 120.45 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  129.55  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.
texts.text: Soybean : About 120.45 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  129.55  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Sugarcane :  About  59.07  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 57.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Sugarcane :  About  59.07  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 57.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Cotton : About 110.03 lakh ha (85% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.97  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.
texts.text: Cotton : About 110.03 lakh ha (85% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.97  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.
